title: Desktop Candy Dispenser
summary: Make your childhood dreams come true by creating a simple and elegant desktop candy-dispensing machine.
description: Make your childhood dreams come true by creating a simple and elegant desktop candy-dispensing machine. The size and shape is easily customizable to fit whatever treats you'd like. Build one for yourself, or put together a couple for a great, universal gift idea.

steps.title: Cut Boards
steps.body: Using a miter saw, cut 1x6, 1x2, and 1x3 boards to length.

steps.title: Cut Sides
steps.body: Use a Multi-Mark™ multi-purpose measuring tool to layout your desired design for the side pieces. Use a jigsaw, scroll saw or router to carefully cut out the outlined side pieces. Create a 3-3/8" diameter circular opening, centered between pieces.

steps.title: Dispenser Circle
steps.body: Use a router, jigsaw, or scroll saw to cut a smooth, 3-1/4" diameter dispenser circle. Determine where you would like the pick-up notch to be located. Use a 1-3/8" diameter forstner drill bit to bore a slightly off-centered semi-circle along the perimeter of the circle. Attach a 3/8" x 1" brass rod or knob in the center of the circle.

steps.title: Connect Sides & Back
steps.body: Place 5-1/2" x 12" back board face-up on your work surface. Align side pieces to the outside edges of back board.

steps.title: Attach Bottom
steps.body: Align 2-1/2" x 5-1/2" bottom board flush to the back side and edges of back board. Allow the bottom board to extend 1" beyond the front face of the side pieces. Apply wood glue to adjoining faces.

steps.title: Attach Lid
steps.body: Align 1-1/2" x 5-1/2" lid on the top edge of the dispenser assembly. Attach using 3/4" x 1/2" brass hinges. (You may need to use a chisel to cut a 1/8" deep recess to accommodate the thickness of the hinges, depending on the style you use.)

steps.title: Place Circle
steps.body: Place dispenser circle in the circular opening between the side pieces.

steps.title: Cut Acrylic Sheet
steps.body: Place 1/16" thick acrylic sheet over the face of the dispenser assembly. Use a Multi-Mark™ measuring tool and permanent marker to trace the outline of the assembly. Use a jigsaw, scroll saw or router to carefully cut the acrylic sheet along the lines drawn. Drill a 1/2" diameter hole to allow the brass knob to protrude.

steps.title: Attach Acrylic
steps.body: Apply a food safe finish, like mineral oil or beeswax to all wood surfaces. Once fully dried, place the acrylic sheet over the front face of the assembly. Lightly clamp in place. Using a 1/16" drill bit, pre-drill 10 holes (5 along either edge) through the acrylic sheet and into the wood beneath. Use a small screwdriver to drive 1/2" long, #4 brass screws.

steps.title: Add Candy
steps.body: Fill the dispenser with your favorite small, hard shelled candy and enjoy!

steps.title: More Tips and Ideas
steps.body: Head to www.PneumaticAddict.com for more DIY tips and ideas.

steps.title: Clamp Pieces Together
steps.body: Apply wood glue to adjoining faces. Use Kreg 3" Wood Project clamps to hold pieces together tightly in place while drying or secure with 1-1/4" brad nails.

steps.title: Clamp While Drying
steps.body: Use a Kreg 16" Auto-Adjust bar clamp to hold pieces in place while drying or secure using 1-1/4" brads.
